purpose: Registry of locally collected related-research articles, with emphasis on evidence useful to the article section 'Data Practices in Related Research'.
path_note: All paths are relative to the root of the vitalii-kaplan/study-visa-open-data GitHub project.
doi_note: DOIs were checked against DOI registration metadata and publisher or repository records. A null DOI means that no DOI was found; it does not prove that none exists.

relevance_scale.meaning: Estimated usefulness to this study, weighted toward public-data sourcing, provenance, normalization, validation, comparability, and methods relevant to 'Data Practices in Related Research'.

articles.title: Immigration challenges and changes in New Zealand: Contemporary policy issues
articles.container_title: New Zealand Journal of Employment Relations
articles.relevance_rationale: Useful New Zealand policy context and administrative migration trends, though student visas and reproducible data construction are not the central focus.

articles.title: Multilateral resistance to migration
articles.relevance_rationale: Offers a strong model for bilateral migration-flow analysis and destination alternatives, but its Spanish migration data are not student-visa administrative data.

articles.title: Best and brightest? The impact of student visa restrictiveness on who attends college in the US
articles.relevance_rationale: Directly combines F-1 refusal-rate variation with linked SAT and enrolment records, making its definitions, linkage choices, and causal design exceptionally useful.

articles.title: A theory of migration: the aspirations-capabilities framework
articles.relevance_rationale: Provides a valuable conceptual framework for interpreting mobility without conflating aspirations and capabilities, but contributes little concrete data practice.

articles.title: Adjustment to university by students in Canada, Spain, and Ecuador: distress and identity issues
articles.relevance_rationale: A cross-national student mental-health study whose survey measures do not address international mobility or visa administrative data.

articles.title: Diversified patterns and future prospects of international student mobility: a multi-level analysis from global and Asian perspectives
articles.relevance_rationale: Its multi-level construction from international secondary databases is highly relevant for comparing geographic scope, variables, and limitations across mobility datasets.

articles.title: Drivers of Mobility of Chinese and Indian Students
articles.relevance_rationale: Offers concise origin-specific trend interpretation, but has limited methodological and provenance detail for the reported statistics.

articles.title: European Higher Education Area, internationalization and students’ mobility in 21st century
articles.container_title: Advances in Social Sciences Research Journal
articles.relevance_rationale: Useful policy and mobility background for Europe, but it is not centred on visa decisions or a reusable cross-national administrative dataset.

articles.title: Higher Education Policy and Migration: The Role of International Student Mobility
articles.relevance_rationale: Connects higher-education policy to student migration using international statistics and clearly warns that student stocks and flows reflect different processes.

articles.title: Immigration Futures: New Zealand in a Global Context
articles.relevance_rationale: Uses New Zealand transition-to-residence data and distinguishes temporary study from residence pathways, but predates the study’s main reporting periods.

articles.title: Inimitable Elements Impacting Student Mobility Among Developing Countries and Developed Countries
articles.container_title: International Journal of Professional Business Review
articles.relevance_rationale: Builds comparative panel data from multiple sources and illustrates source harmonization choices, although its outcome is student flow rather than visa processing.

articles.title: Institutional Determinants of Higher Education Students’ International Mobility within the Erasmus Programme Countries
articles.relevance_rationale: Demonstrates cross-country variable selection and institutional-data comparison for Erasmus mobility, useful as a contrast to administrative visa outcomes.

articles.title: Introducing Academia Global and Public Health
articles.relevance_rationale: An editorial introducing a public-health journal; it contains no student-mobility, visa, or related data practice.

articles.title: Migration of Students—A Comparative Study among Different Countries of the World
articles.container_title: International Journal of Trend in Scientific Research and Development
articles.relevance_rationale: Reports comparative web-sourced student counts, but weak provenance and unclear definitions limit its value as a model of data practice.

articles.title: Migration of Students from India and China: A Comparative View
articles.relevance_rationale: Provides useful India–China trend comparisons and policy interpretation, but does not document a reusable visa-outcome data pipeline.

articles.title: Motivations and Constraints of Moving Abroad for Indian Students
articles.container_title: Journal of International Migration and Integration
articles.relevance_rationale: Its documented survey and interview design provides a useful primary-data contrast, while measuring intentions rather than visa applications or decisions.

articles.title: International Student Study Destination Choices: The Role of Market Regulation and Government Visa Policies in Global Education Markets—A Systematic Literature Review
articles.relevance_rationale: Directly reviews visa-policy effects on destination choice, but the local manuscript lacks publication metadata, a DOI, and a transparent reproducible review protocol.

articles.title: Shifting International Student Mobility Directions and Factors Influencing Students’ Higher Education Destination Choices
articles.container_title: Journal of Higher Education Policy and Leadership Studies
articles.relevance_rationale: Synthesizes mobility trends and destination factors across major destinations, but relies mainly on literature rather than a documented administrative dataset.

articles.title: The Role of Regional Differences in Immigration: The Case of OECD Countries
articles.relevance_rationale: Shows use of OECD DIOC cross-tabulations and regional controls, but focuses on immigrant populations rather than students or visa decisions.

articles.title: The Factors of Studying Abroad for Indian Students
articles.container_title: Journal of Emerging Technologies and Innovative Research
articles.relevance_rationale: Mentions Australian student-visa demand and combines focus groups with a survey, but source definitions and statistical provenance are too limited for close reuse.

articles.title: The Global Mobility Divide: How Visa Policies Have Evolved over Time
articles.container_title: Journal of Ethnic and Migration Studies
articles.relevance_rationale: Presents a purpose-built country-pair visa-waiver database for two time points and discusses coding coverage and inequality—an excellent methodological comparator.

articles.title: The quality of immigrant source country educational outcomes: Do they matter in the receiving country?
articles.relevance_rationale: Carefully links Canadian census microdata to country-level education indices, but concerns immigrant earnings rather than international students or visa processing.

articles.title: The determinants of United Kingdom student visa demand from developing countries
articles.relevance_rationale: Directly models official UK student-visa applications from 89 origins over 2001–2008 and documents panel construction, covariates, and interpretation.

articles.title: The Impact of Economic Selection Policy on Labour Market Outcomes for Degree-Qualified Migrants in Canada and Australia
articles.relevance_rationale: A detailed Canada–Australia policy and outcomes comparison that helps frame definitional differences, though it studies skilled migrants after selection rather than student visas.

articles.title: The student migration transition: an empirical investigation into the nexus between development and international student migration
articles.relevance_rationale: Constructs a 2003–2018 UNESCO student-mobility panel and clearly handles origins, destinations, development measures, and coverage limits.

articles.title: Three waves of international student mobility (1999–2020)
articles.relevance_rationale: Provides a helpful periodization of policy shocks and enrolment trends, but is primarily interpretive and less explicit about reproducible data preparation.

articles.title: Unequal access to foreign spaces: how states use visa restrictions to regulate mobility in a globalized world
articles.container_title: Transactions of the Institute of British Geographers
articles.relevance_rationale: A foundational cross-national visa-restriction analysis whose country-pair coding and attention to unequal access inform comparison, despite not isolating student visas.

articles.title: Tendências na educação moderna
articles.container_title: Revista on line de Política e Gestão Educacional
articles.relevance_rationale: Combines a literature review with a 37-expert ranking of mobility factors; useful context, but not evidence about official visa counts or harmonization.

articles.title: Determinants of International Student Migration
articles.relevance_rationale: A central quantitative study of bilateral international-student flows with detailed panel-data construction and destination/source covariates.

articles.title: The global visa cost divide: How and why the price for travel permits varies worldwide
articles.relevance_rationale: Documents manual collection and coding of country-pair visa costs across seven categories, including student visas, with unusually relevant provenance challenges.

articles.title: Navigating visa inequities: mobility as privilege in academia—‘You are not supposed to be here’
articles.relevance_rationale: Adds an important qualitative account of visa inequity in academic mobility, but its autoethnographic evidence does not guide administrative-data construction.

articles.title: Visa policy and international student migration: evidence from Canada
articles.relevance_rationale: Directly uses Canadian administrative college and visa-policy data, distinguishes processing time from approval rates, and transparently defines a causal comparison.

articles.title: A Trend Analysis of the Challenges of International Students Over 21 Years
articles.relevance_rationale: Its database search, screening, and content analysis are relevant review practices, but the topic is post-arrival student challenges rather than visa statistics.

articles.title: A quantitative assessment of potential benefits and challenges of international researcher mobility for home and host countries: evidence from the Chinese Scholarship Council programmes
articles.relevance_rationale: Its scholarship-programme and bibliometric linkage offers a useful data-integration example, but researcher mobility is outside student-visa outcome scope.
